data.title: Lennar Non-GAAP EPS of $2.14 beats by $0.43, revenue of $7.63B beats by $200M
data.description:
• Lennar press release (NYSE:LEN): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.14 beats by $0.43.
  • Revenue of $7.63B (+4.4% Y/Y) beats by $200M.
  • New orders increased 1% to 18,355 homes; new orders dollar value decreased 4% to $7.4 billion
  • Backlog of 13,145 homes with a dollar value of $5.8 billion
  • Deliveries increased 6% to 17,834 homes.

  • Guidance

    The following are the Company's expected results of its homebuilding and financial services activities for the second quarter of 2025:

    New Orders

    22,500 - 23,500

    Deliveries

    19,500 - 20,500

    Average Sales Price

    $390,000 - $400,000

    Gross Margin % on Home Sales

    approximately 18%

    S,G&A as a % of Home Sales

    8.0% - 8.2%

    Financial Services Operating Earnings

    $135 million - $145 million.
  • Shares -2.56% AH.

data.title: Ashford Hospitality reports property-level jobs cuts to reduce costs, boost EBITDA
data.description:
Ashford Hospitality Trust (NYSE:AHT) said on Thursday that Remington, its largest property manager, implemented property-level headcount reductions, reduced travel expenses, changed its paid time-off policy for field associates, and reduced contracted services and centralized expenses to increase its hotel EBITDA.

The moves are expected to boost its annual run-rate EBITDA by $50M and "significantly" increase shareholder value. The cost cuts are expected to add more than $11M in incremental hotel EBiTDA. Combined with previously announced initiatives, the moves are expected to contribute more than $30M per year in incremental EBITDA towards its $50M goal.

The company's statement didn't disclose the number of jobs affected or whether it expects to record charges related to the cost cuts. The company didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

Ashford Hospitality (NYSE:AHT) stock fell 0.7% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Nucor issues below-consensus guidance for Q1 earnings
data.description:
Nucor (NYSE:NUE) -2.8% post-market Thursday after issuing downside guidance for Q1 earnings, expecting adjusted EPS of $0.50-$0.60, well below $1.06/share analyst consensus estimate as well as $1.22 in Q4 2024 and $3.46 in the year-earlier quarter.

Nucor (NYSE:NUE) said it expects Q1 earnings in its steel mills segment will be in line with Q4 2024, earnings in the steel products segment likely will decrease compared to Q4 due primarily to lower average selling prices, and earnings of the raw materials segment are seen decreasing from Q4 due to lower margins at its DRI facilities.

Included in Q1 non-adjusted guidance range are ~$16M, or $0.05/share, in estimated one-time charges related to the closure of two facilities in the steel products segment.

data.title: Allurion surges on weight loss data from gastric balloon, GLP-1 combo treatment
data.description:
Microcap Allurion Technologies (NYSE:ALUR) surged ~31% in after-hours trading Thursday after posting preliminary data on weight loss associated with its gastric balloon in combination with Novo Nordisk's (NVO) GLP-1 semaglutide.

A study enrolled 52 individuals who already had the swallowable Allurion Balloon in their system for a month. Patients were then started on 0.25 mg semaglutide which was increased to a maximum of 1.0mg over the next six months. 

At eight months of receiving combination treatment, average total body weight loss was 20.3%, and lean body mass increased by 15% from 59.6% to 68.5%.

"These results indicate that a combination approach increases weight loss and that by using lower doses of GLP-1s, patients do not experience as many side effects—including muscle wasting—and have improved adherence," said Luigi Flagiello, a bariatric surgeon at Clinica Ruesch in Naples, Italy, who oversaw the study. "Patients may also be benefiting from two distinct physiological impacts: satiety induction from the balloon and reduced hunger from the GLP-1."

Semaglutide is the active ingredient in Novo's blockbuster obesity drug Wegovy. Data on that drug released in January found that at the 2.5 mg dose, patients lost an average of 17.5% of their weight over 72 weeks.

data.title: Valneva reports FY results
data.description:
• Valneva press release (NASDAQ:VALN): FY Total revenues were €169.6 million for the year ended December 31, 2024 compared to €153.7 million for the year ended December 31, 2023, an increase of 10%.
  • Net loss of €12.2 million compared to a 2023 net loss of €101.4 million, including proceeds from the Priority Review Voucher sale.

  • Financial OutlookThe Company provides the following guidance on its future results:

    • Product sales expected to grow to €170-180 million in 2025, driving positive cash-flows for the overall commercial business
    • Total revenues expected to reach €180-190 million in 2025
    • Total R&D investments expected between €90 - €100 million in 2025, which will be partially offset by grant fundings and anticipated R&D tax credits
    • Continued stringent focus on cash management supporting sufficient cash runway to reach key inflection points; targeting more than 50% lower operating cash burn in 2025.

data.title: Sunoco prices upsized private offering of senior notes
data.description:
• Sunoco (NYSE:SUN) said on Thursday that it has priced at 100% a private offering of 6.250% senior notes due 2033 in an aggregate principal amount of $1B.
  • This offering was upsized from an initial offering size of $750M aggregate principal amount.
  • The sale of the notes is expected to settle on March 31, 2025.
  • Sunoco intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to repay indebtedness, including by redeeming in full NuStar Logistics, L.P.'s 5.750% senior notes due 2025 and repaying a portion of the outstanding borrowings under Sunoco's revolving credit facility.
  • Source: Press Release
